purpose: Detects regime mispricing by comparing implied growth, inflation, and disruption assumptions across equities, rates, credit, and currencies
components: Winner-take-all equilibrium as fragile partial reconciliation
current_assessment: Roughly 60% genuine inconsistency across asset classes and 40% artifact of correlated estimation errors from shared input data. Winner-take-all equilibrium partially reconciles: equities right about Mag-7, rates right about broad economy, credit right about no systemic stress. The inconsistency is real but its magnitude as an independent signal is overstated.
limitations: Equity ERP, bond r*, and credit spreads share common input data, partially deflating their independent disagreement
Different market participants with different mandates process shared data through different loss functions
Model specification choices affect measured inconsistency
No formal framework to weight asset class signals against each other
